1.title: welcome
1.content:
Welcome to our study
${parameters.study_name}!

Dear participants,

Thank you for your interest in participating in our study on human judgment and decision making.

The entire study takes about ${this.parameters.time}. You will receive a reward of ${parameters.pay}.

Your Prolific-ID was automatically saved and you will automatically be redirected back to Prolific at the end of the experiment.

To complete this study you need to use a PC, laptop, or tablet. It is not possible to complete the study with a smartphone.

You will now receive some information about the study, data protection and our rules for participation. Please read the information carefully to decide whether you would like to take part in the study.

Note: Depending on your browser and screen size, you may have to scroll down to the bottom of a page to read all the information or to go to the next page. You can change the general size of the content in your browser window using the key combination STRG + +/- or for Mac ⌘ + +/-



Please click on ${parameters.btn_label} to continue

${parameters.footer}

messageHandlers.code:
//pre load images
this.options.media.images = Object.values(this.options.files)


// Define Study Parameters
this.parameters.study_name   = "Estimating Life Expenctancy in Countries: A Window into Human Judgment and Decision-Making"
this.parameters.bst          = 5000
this.parameters.pay          = "£5"
this.parameters.time         = "approx. 30 min"
this.parameters.footer       = "Dr. David Izydorczyk | izydorczyk@uni-mannheim.de | Chair of Experimental Psychology | University of Mannheim"
this.parameters.btn_label    = "Next"

3.content:
${parameters.trial+1}/12

What is the life expectancy in this country (in years)?

   years

messageHandlers.code:
let nr_correct = this.state.nr_correct 
let est        = this.state.est
let correct    = this.state.crit


if((est >= (correct*0.9)) && (est <= (correct*1.1))){
  nr_correct += 1
} 

this.state.nr_correct = nr_correct

3.title: training_est_trial

6.content:
Information on the study

Aim of the study:

This study is being conducted as part of a project funded by the German Research Foundation and led by Dr. David Izydorczyk (izydorczyk@uni-mannheim.de) from the Department of Experimental Psychology at the University of Mannheim. We want to gain new insights into human decision-making and judgment processes. In particular, we are interested in how people estimate or judge features of specific objects, which allows us to develop and test theories about how humans process information and navigate complex environments, which is essential for improving our understanding of human cognition and behavior.

Procedure of the study:

Your main task in this study will be to judge the life expectancy in different countries. The task will be explained to you in more detail later.

The study is expected to take ${this.parameters.time} to complete. It is very important that you work on the study in one go and do not take any breaks. Please only start when you are undisturbed for the next ${this.parameters.time}.

Benefits and risks:

By participating in the study, you will contribute to scientific research. You will also receive a reward of ${parameters.pay} upon completing the study.

There are no known health risks associated with the research methods used in this study. Participating in our study is voluntary. Naturally, you are free to end your participation at any time, without stating any reasons. Aborting the study by returning your submission or by letting it time out won’t cause you any disadvantages or affect your approval rating.

Please understand that you can receive credit for your participation only if you have completed all parts of our study.

Terms of participation

You can only participate in the study if you fulfil the following criterion. You will be asked on the following pages to confirm that you fulfil this criterion. You can only participate in the study if you:

  • are at least 18 years old

Policy for Rejecting Submissions & Payment:

We will use objective measures to assess your compliance with the task instructions, for example by using so-called “attention checks” (AC) to check that you have read the instructions. Please note that you cannot qualify for payment if you fail an attention check.

If you experience any technical difficulties with our website, please return your submission and contact us through Prolific’s messaging system. We will do our best to identify the cause of the encountered problem. Please understand, however, that we cannot be held accountable for dropouts caused by outdated browsers, incompatibilities between browsers and our study, an unstable internet connection or the like.

Please return your submission if you cannot complete the study (for whatever reasons). Note however, that you qualify for full payment only if you complete the entire study until the end.

After you have fully completed the study, we will process your answers as fast as possible. Please understand that it might take us up to 72 hours to reward you for your participation.

Type and Use of the Data Collected:

The data collected in this study mainly refers to your responses in the judgment task. We will also ask you to provide some demographic information, such as gender, age, and level of education. Note that we will never reject submissions based on your responses to these questions.

All data collected in this study will be processed under Article 13 of the General Data Protection Regulation EU (GDPR) and used exclusively for scientific purposes. All your data will only be processed by employees of the Chair of Experimental Psychology at the University of Mannheim involved in the project.

Your data is collected and saved in a fully anonymized manner and solely linked to your anonymous Prolific ID. Therefore, it is not possible to link your data to any personally identifiable information (such as your name or e-mail address). If you wish your data to be deleted after your participation, please withdraw your consent by returning your submission or contact us via Prolific’s anonymous messaging system before we review (and reward) your submission (i.e., within max. 72 hours). After reviewing your submission, we will immediately remove your Prolific ID from your data so that it will be no longer possible to delete your data.

Declaration of consent:

By ticking the following box "I consent to my personal data being processed in accordance with the information provided here", you confirm the following statements:

  • I have read and understood the detailed data protection information.
  • I am at least 18 years old.
  • I have been informed about the objectives and procedure of the study in accordance with Art. 13 GDPR.
  • I have been informed that there are no known risks associated with the research methods of this study.
  • I am taking part in this study voluntarily. I can withdraw my consent to participate or discontinue participation in the study at any time and without giving reasons. Withdrawing my consent to participate or cancelling my participation in the study will have no adverse consequences for me.
  • I agree that the anonymous data collected as part of this study may be stored in computers for 10 years and processed for scientific purposes.
  • I agree that my anonymised data from this study may be published with the study results in specialist journals or on open science portals for exclusively scientific purposes. The data will be published in such a form that no conclusions can be drawn about you as a person.

    • I consent to my personal data being processed in accordance with the information provided here.


${parameters.footer}
6.title: consent

messageHandlers.code: this.data.phase = "training"

7.title: instructions
7.content:
The task

This study examines how people estimate the life expectancy (in years) in different countries, where life expectancy at birth reflects the overall mortality level of a population. It is the average number of years that a person may expect to live, from birth to death. The task consists of two phases: a training phase and a testing phase.

Training phase

During the training phase, you will see names of 12 different countries. Your task is to estimate the life expectancy in years for each country.

After each judgment, you will receive feedback about the actual value. Try to learn and memorise these values as best you can, since this information will help you in the testing phase.

The training phase consists of 5 to 10 blocks, with each block containing 12 trials (one trial per country).

Testing phase

Your task in the testing phase will then be to estimate the life expectancy of 80 different countries. There will be no feedback in this phase.


Please note the following again:

  • Please do not take any notes on the tasks.
  • Please do not take any photos of the tasks.
  • Please do not look up any answers to the tasks.
  • Please do not ask other people for help.

${parameters.footer}

10.content:
${parameters.trial+1}/12




has a life expectancy of

${parameters.crit} years

53.content:
Requirements

Thank you for your willingness to participate in this study! During this study, we ask you to comply with the following requirements:

  1. Please make sure that you can work undisturbed and without interruptions for the duration of the study and that your mobile phone is set to silent (without vibration) or switched off. Please read all instructions carefully.

  2. To ensure that everything is displayed correctly, please maximise the size of your web browser so that it covers your entire screen ( F11 in most browsers).

  3. During the study, please do not use the back button or refresh buttons of your web browser. This will result in the study being cancelled.

  4. If possible, please complete the study in a single session and do not leave the study to attend to other tasks.

  5. As this study requires your full attention, we ask you to carry out the study in an environment that is free from noise and distraction. Please do not talk to anyone during the study and stay in a quiet room.

  6. So only start the study if you can work on it continuously and undisturbed.

${parameters.footer}
53.title: requirements

messageHandlers.code: this.data.phase = "testing"

66.title: instructions
66.content:
The task

You finished the training phase !


Your task in the following testing phase will be to estimate the life expectancy (in years) of 80 different countries, including the 12 countries from the training phase. There will be no feedback in this phase.

In three of the trials, you will shown a basketball instead of a country. In these Attention Check trials, enter ‘999’ (i.e. the number 999) as the answer.

It is likely that you will again be uncertain. In these cases, however, please make an effort to give a realistic estimate for each question. Answer even if you find it very difficult.


Please note the following again:

  • Please do not take any notes on the tasks.
  • Please do not take any photos of the tasks.
  • Please do not look up any answers to the tasks.
  • Please do not ask other people for help.

${parameters.footer}

messageHandlers.code: this.data.phase = "controle_demographic"

70.title: instructions
70.content:
Information on the study

You are now finished with the main part of the study. Thank you very much for your efforts!

Finally, we have a few questions about the course of the study and about you personally.

Please answer honestly. Your answers will not affect your payment.

${parameters.footer}

Please answer honestly. There are no disadvantages for you if you have not worked on the study seriously, but it helps us a lot with determining the data quality.

81.title: debriefing & end
81.content:
End

You have made it! Thank you for your participation! Finally, we would like to inform you about the background to the study.

This experiment was the same for all participants and aimed to investigate how people estimate the life expectancy in different countries. Understanding how individuals judge specific object features, helps us develop and refine theories about human judgment and information processing. Your responses contribute to research on cognitive mechanisms underlying estimation processes.

Please click on Finish this study, to submit your data and complete the study. You will then be automatically redirected back to Prolific.

${parameters.footer}
